volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Captives (Section 3.4.653 - Section 4.1.770)

sections.label: Section 3.4.653
sections.original: Satin med illi hodie scelesti capti ceperunt dolo ?
sections.translation: And have these two rascally captives really deceived me this day with their tricks ? The other one pretended that he was the servant , and this one that he himself was the master .

sections.label: Section 3.4.655
sections.original: nuculeum amisi , retinui pignori putamina .
sections.translation: I ’ ve lost the kernel ; for a security , I ’ ve left the shell . To such a degree have they imposed upon me [*] , both on this side and that , with their trickeries . Still , this fellow shall never have the laugh against me . Colaphus , Cordalio , Corax [*] ( to the SLAVES ) , go you away and bring out the thongs .

sections.label: Section 3.4.658b
sections.original: Num lignatum mittimur ?
sections.translation: Are we to be sent to gather faggots [*] ? ( The SLAVES go and bring the thongs from the house .)

sections.label: Section 3.5.659
sections.original: Inícite huic manicas ⋯ mastigiae .
sections.translation: Put the manacles on this whipp ’ d villain .

sections.label: Section 3.5.660
sections.original: Quid hoc ést negoti ? quid ego deliqui ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ? What have I done wrong ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.660b
sections.original: Rogas ,
sections.translation: Do you ask the question ? You weeder and sower of villanies , and in especial their reaper .

sections.label: Section 3.5.662
sections.original: Non occatorem dicere audebas prius ?
sections.translation: Ought you not to have ventured to say the harrower first ? For countrymen always harrow before they weed .

sections.label: Section 3.5.664
sections.original: At ut cónfidenter ⋯ mihi contra astitit .
sections.translation: Why , with what assurance he stands before me .

sections.label: Section 3.5.665
sections.original: Decet innocentem servom átque innoxium
sections.translation: It ’ s proper for a servant , innocent and guiltless , to be full of confidence , most especially before his master .

sections.label: Section 3.5.667
sections.original: Adstringite isti sultis vehementer manus .
sections.translation: Bind this fellow ’ s hands tightly , will you .

sections.label: Section 3.5.668
sections.original: Tuós sum , tu has quidem vel praecidi iube .
sections.translation: I am your own — do you command them to be cut off even . But what is the matter on account of which you blame me ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.670
sections.original: Quia me meamque rem , quod in te uno fuit ,
sections.translation: Because me and my fortunes , so far as in you singly lay , by your rascally and knavish stratagems you have rent in pieces , and have distracted my affairs and spoiled all my resources and my plans , in that you ’ ve thus robbed me of Philocrates by your devices ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.675
sections.original: illum esse servom credidi , te liberum ;
sections.translation: I thought that he was the slave , you the free man . So did you say yourselves , and in this way did you change names between you .

sections.label: Section 3.5.677b
sections.original: Fateor , omnia
sections.translation: I confess that all was done so , as you say , and that by a stratagem he has got away from you , through my aid and cleverness ;

sections.label: Section 3.5.680
sections.original: an , obsecro hercle te , id nunc suscenses mihi ?
sections.translation: and prithee , now , do you blame me for that , i ’ faith ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.681
sections.original: At cum cruciatu maxumo id factumst tuo .
sections.translation: Why , it has been done with your extreme torture for the consequence .

sections.label: Section 3.5.682
sections.original: Dum ne ob male facta , peream , parvi aestumo .
sections.translation: So I don ’ t die by reason of my misdeeds , I care but little . If I do die here , then he returns not , as he said he would ; but when I ’ m dead , this act will be remembered to my honor ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.685
sections.original: me meum erum captum ex servitute atque hostibus
sections.translation: that I caused my captive master to return from slavery and the foe , a free man , to his father in his native land ; and that I preferred rather to expose my own life to peril , than that he should be undone .

sections.label: Section 3.5.689
sections.original: Facito ergo ut Acherunti clueas gloria .
sections.translation: Take care , then , to enjoy that fame at Acheron .

sections.label: Section 3.5.690
sections.original: Qui per virtutem , periit , at non interit .
sections.translation: He who dies for virtue ’ s sake , still does not perish .

sections.label: Section 3.5.691
sections.original: Quando ego te exemplis pessumis cruciavero
sections.translation: When I ’ ve tortured you in the most severe manner , and for your schemes put you to death , let them say either that you have perished or that you have died ; so long as you do die , I don ’ t think it matters if they say you live .

sections.label: Section 3.5.695
sections.original: Pol si istuc faxis , haud sine poena feceris ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , if you do do so , you ’ ll do it not without retribution , if he shall return here , as I trust that he will return .

sections.label: Section 3.5.697
sections.original: Pro di immortales , nunc ego teneo , nunc scio
sections.translation: O ye immortal Gods ! I understand it now ; now I know what the case really is . My friend Philocrates is at liberty with his father , in his native land . ’ Tis well ;

sections.label: Section 3.5.700
sections.original: nec quisquam est mihi , aeque melius cui velim .
sections.translation: nor have I any person to whom I could so readily wish well . But this thing grieves me , that I ’ ve done this person a bad turn , who now on account of me and my talking is in chains .

sections.label: Section 3.5.703
sections.original: Votuin te quicquam mi hodie falsum proloqui ?
sections.translation: Did I not forbid you this day to utter anything false to me ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.704
sections.original: Votuisti .
sections.translation: You did forbid me .

sections.label: Section 3.5.704b
sections.original: Cur es ausus mentiri mihi ?
sections.translation: Why did you dare to tell me lies ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.705
sections.original: Quia vera obessent illi quoi operam dabam :
sections.translation: Because the truth would have prejudiced him whom I was serving ; now falsehood has advantaged him .

sections.label: Section 3.5.706b
sections.original: At tibi oberunt .
sections.translation: But it will prejudice yourself .

sections.label: Section 3.5.706c
sections.original: Optumest .
sections.translation: ’ Tis very good . Still , I have saved my master , whom I rejoice at being saved , to whom my elder master had assigned me as a protector . But do you think that this was wrongly done ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.709b
sections.original: Pessume .
sections.translation: Most wrongfully .

sections.label: Section 3.5.710
sections.original: At ego aio recte , qui abs te sorsum sentio .
sections.translation: But I , who disagree with you , say , rightly . For consider , if any slave of yours had done this for your son , what thanks you would have given him . Would you have given that slave his freedom or not ? Would not that slave have been in highest esteem with you ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.715
sections.original: responde .
sections.translation: Answer me that .

sections.label: Section 3.5.715b
sections.original: Opinor .
sections.translation: I think so .

sections.label: Section 3.5.715c
sections.original: Cur ergo iratus mihi es ?
sections.translation: Why , then , are you angry with me ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.716
sections.original: Quia illi fuisti quam mihi fidelior .
sections.translation: Because you have proved more faithful to him than to myself .

sections.label: Section 3.5.717
sections.original: Quid ? tu una nocte postulavisti et die
sections.translation: How now ? Did you expect , in a single night and day , for yourself to teach me — a person just made captive , a recent slave , and in his noviciate — that I should rather consult your interest than his ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.720
sections.original: quam illi , quicum una a puero aetatem exegeram ?
sections.translation: with whom from childhood I have passed my life ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.721
sections.original: Ergo ab eo petito gratiam istam . ducite ,
sections.translation: Seek , then , thanks from him for that . ( To the SLAVES .) Take him where he may receive weighty and thick fetters , thence , after that , you shall go to the quarries for cutting stone . There , while the others are digging out eight stones , unless

sections.label: Section 3.5.725
sections.original: cotidiano sesquiopus confeceris ,
sections.translation: you daily do half as much work again , you shall have the name of the six-hundred-stripe man [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.5.727
sections.original: Per deos atque homines ego te obtestor , Hegio ,
sections.translation: By Gods and men , I do entreat you , Hegio , not to destroy this man .

sections.label: Section 3.5.728b
sections.original: Curabitur ;
sections.translation: He shall be taken all care of [*] For at night , fastened with chains , he shall be watched ;

sections.label: Section 3.5.730
sections.original: interdius sub terra lapides eximet :
sections.translation: in the daytime , beneath the ground , he shall be getting out stone . For many a day will I torture him ; I ’ ll not respite him for a single day .

sections.label: Section 3.5.732
sections.original: Certumne est tíbi istuc ?
sections.translation: Is that settled by you ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.732b
sections.original: Non moriri certius .
sections.translation: Not more settled that I shall die . ( To the SLAVES .) Take him away this instant to Hippolytus , the blacksmith ; bid thick fetters to be rivetted on him .

sections.label: Section 3.5.735
sections.original: inde extra portam ad meum libertum Cordalum
sections.translation: From there let him be led outside the gate to my freedman , Cordalus , at the stone-quarries . And tell him that I desire this man so to be treated , that he mayn ’ t be in any respect worse off than he who is the most severely treated .

sections.label: Section 3.5.739
sections.original: Cur ego te invito me esse salvom postulem ?
sections.translation: Why , since you are unwilling , do I desire myself to survive ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.740
sections.original: periclum vitae meae tuo stat periculo .
sections.translation: At your own hazard is the risk of my life . After death , no evil have I to apprehend in death . Though I should live even to extreme age , still , short is the space for enduring what you threaten me with . Farewell and prosper although you are deserving for me to say otherwise .

sections.label: Section 3.5.745
sections.original: tu , Aristophontes , de me ut meruisti , ita vale ;
sections.translation: You , Aristophontes , as you have deserved of me , so fare you ; for on your account has this befallen me .

sections.label: Section 3.5.746b
sections.original: Abducite .
sections.translation: Carry him off .

sections.label: Section 3.5.747
sections.original: At unum hoc quaeso , si huc rebitet Philocrates ,
sections.translation: But this one thing I beg , that , if Philocrates should come back here , you will give me an opportunity of meeting him .

sections.label: Section 3.5.749
sections.original: Periistis , nisi hunc iam e conspectu abducitis .
sections.translation: At your peril , if you don ’ t this instant remove him from my sight .

sections.label: Section 3.5.750
sections.original: Vis haec quidem hercle est , et trahi et trudi simul .—
sections.translation: I ’ troth , this really is violence [*] , to be both dragged and pushed at the same time . ( He is borne off by the SLAVES .)

sections.label: Section 3.5.751
sections.original: Illic ést abductus recta in phylacam , ut dignus est .
sections.translation: He has been led off straight to prison [*] as he deserves . Let no one presume to attempt such an enterprise . Had it not been for you who discovered this to me ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.755
sections.original: usque offrenatum suis me ductarent dolis .
sections.translation: still would they have been leading me by the bridle with their tricks . Now am I resolved henceforth never to trust any person in anything . This once I have been deceived enough ; I did hope , to my sorrow , that I had rescued my son from slavery . That hope has forsaken me . I lost one son ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.760
sections.original: puerum quadrimum quem mihi servos surpuit ,
sections.translation: whom , a child in his fourth year , a slave stole from me ; and , indeed , never since have I found either slave or son ; the elder one has fallen in the hands of the enemy . What guilt is this of mine ? As though I had become the father of children for the purpose of being childless . ( To AISTOPHONTES .) Follow this way . I ’ ll conduct you back where you were .

sections.label: Section 3.5.765
sections.original: miserere certum est , quia mei miseret neminem .—
sections.translation: I ’ m determined to have pity upon no one , since no one has pity upon me .

sections.label: Section 3.5.766
sections.original: Exauspicavi ex vinclis . nunc intellego
sections.translation: Forth from my chains with evil omen did I come ; now I perceive that with like ill omen to my bonds I must return .

sections.label: Section 4.1.768
sections.original: Iúppiter supreme , servas me measque auges opes ,
sections.translation: Supreme Jove ! thou dost preserve me , and dost augment my means . Plenty , extreme and sumptuous , dost thou present to me ;

sections.label: Section 4.1.770
sections.original: laudém lucrum , ludum iocum , festivitatem ferias ,
sections.translation: celebrity , profit , enjoyment , mirth , festivity , holidays , sights , provisions , carousings , abundance , joyousness . And to no man have I now determined with myself to go a-begging ; for I ’ m able either to profit my friend or to destroy my enemy , to such extent has this delightful day heaped delights upon me in its delightfulness .
